political.s: Infiniti USA, as a subsidiary of Nissan, does not disclose independent political contributions. Parent company Nissan engages in standard automotive industry lobbying through trade associations.

charity.s: Infiniti has donated over $8 million to charities through its NCAA partnership since 2009, with focus on cancer research and coaching charities. Individual dealerships support local community causes including food banks and youth organizations.
charity.focus: Cancer research, youth programs, community development

environment.s: Infiniti Motor Company Ltd. received a DitchCarbon sustainability score of 62/100 and has published science-based carbon reduction targets aligned with SBTi guidelines. The brand offers fuel-efficient and hybrid vehicles and invests in electric vehicle development.

labor.s: Not disclosed - Limited public information available on Infiniti USA specific labor practices, OSHA records, or NLRB filings. Parent company Nissan operates manufacturing facilities in multiple countries.

dei.s: Infiniti Canada publicly commits to diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives and has received Great Place to Work certification for five consecutive years. Has partnered with Accelerate Auto to support Black talent development in automotive industry.

animals.s: Not applicable - Infiniti is an automotive manufacturer with no animal testing practices.

guns.s: Not applicable - Infiniti is an automotive manufacturer that does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: Infiniti respects user privacy and is committed to protecting personal data collected through vehicles and platforms. Data retention policies disclosed in privacy notices; INFINITI InTouch services require enrollment and subscription terms.

execPay.s: Not disclosed - Infiniti USA is a private subsidiary of Nissan; executive compensation data not publicly available. CEO pay ratio not disclosed separately from parent company.
